availableCommands.name: update-config
availableCommands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

availableCommands.name: claude-api
availableCommands.description:
Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

availableCommands.name: pair-agent
availableCommands.description:
Pair a remote AI agent with your browser. One command generates a setup key and
prints instructions the other agent can follow to connect. Works with OpenClaw,
Hermes, Codex, Cursor, or any agent that can make HTTP requests. The remote agent
gets its own tab with scoped access (read+write by default, admin on request).
Use when asked to "pair agent", "connect agent", "share browser", "remote browser",
"let another agent use my browser", or "give browser access". (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "pair agent", "connect agent", "share my browser", "remote browser access". (user)

availableCommands.name: devex-review

exist (the boomerang: plan said 3 minutes, reality says 8). Use when asked to
"test the DX", "DX audit", "developer experience test", or "try the
onboarding". Proactively suggest after shipping a developer-facing feature. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "dx audit", "test the developer experience", "try the onboarding", "developer experience test". (user)

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Commands

```bash
bun install                          # Install dependencies
bun test                             # Run all tests (bun:test)
bun test tests/memory.test.ts        # Run single test file
bun run src/main.ts                  # Interactive CLI REPL (default)
bun run src/main.ts serve            # Daemon mode (connectors + scheduler)
```

## Architecture

Hub-and-spoke design. `Remi` (core.ts) is the hub orchestrator that routes messages between **Connectors** (input) and **Providers** (AI backends).

```
Connector → IncomingMessage → Remi → Provider.send() → AgentResponse → Connector.reply()
```

**Message flow in Remi._process():**
1. Resolve session (chatId → sessionId mapping for multi-turn)
2. Route to provider (with fallback on failure)
3. Append interaction to daily journal
4. Return AgentResponse

**Key interfaces** (in `*/base.ts`):
- `Provider`: `send()`, `healthCheck()`, `name` — AI backend interface
- `Connector`: `start(handler)`, `stop()`, `reply()`, `name` — input adapter interface

**Providers**: `ClaudeCLIProvider` uses Claude Code subscription via long-running subprocess with bidirectional JSONL streaming. No API key needed.

**Connectors**: `CLIConnector` (dev REPL).

**Memory**: Dual-layer markdown files at `~/.remi/memory/`. `MemoryStore` handles read/write with automatic `.versions/` backups. Context is loaded natively by Claude Code via CLAUDE.md + MEMORY.md; recall MCP tool provides on-demand search.

**Scheduler**: Pure async, runs heartbeat (provider health) + daily memory compaction (summarize yesterday's notes → append to long-term memory) + cleanup (old dailies/versions).

**Config**: `RemiConfig` loaded from env vars > `remi.toml` > defaults. Search path: `./remi.toml`, `~/.remi/remi.toml`.

## Conventions

- Full async/await — no threads, no sync blocking in async paths
- TypeScript strict mode
- Interfaces over class inheritance for loose coupling
- Plain objects + interfaces for data types (IncomingMessage, AgentResponse, ToolDefinition, configs)
- AsyncLock per chatId prevents race conditions in concurrent message handling
- Bun runtime, `bun:test` for testing
- `node:fs` sync APIs for memory store (file I/O), `Bun.spawn()` for subprocesses

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the config system. Let me compile my findings:

## Config System Analysis - Remi TypeScript Project

Based on my thorough search, here's a complete breakdown of the RemiConfig system:

### **1. Core Type Definitions** (`/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/config.ts`)

**Main RemiConfig Interface** (lines 196-226):
```
- provider: ProviderConfig
- feishu: FeishuConfig
- bytedanceSso?: ByteDanceSSOConfig
- tokenSync: TokenSyncRuleConfig[]
- scheduler: SchedulerConfig (deprecated, for migration)
- scheduledSkills: ScheduledSkillConfig[] (deprecated, for migration)
- cronJobs: CronJobConfig[] (new unified scheduler)
- services: ServiceConfig[]
- botMenu: BotMenuConfig
- proxy: ProxyConfig
- embedding?: EmbeddingConfig
- google?: GoogleConfig
- tracing: TracingConfig
- memoryDir: string
- pidFile: string
- logLevel: string
- contextWarnThreshold: number
- queueDir: string
- sessionsFile: string
```

**Sub-Interfaces**:
- **ProviderConfig** (13-21): name, fallback, allowedTools, model, timeout, apiKey, baseUrl
- **FeishuConfig** (23-34): appId, appSecret, verificationToken, encryptKey, port, domain, connectionMode, userAccessToken, triggerUserIds
- **CronJobConfig** (65-84): id, name, handler, enabled, cron/every/at (mutually exclusive), tz, timeoutMs, deleteAfterRun, handlerConfig
- **ServiceConfig** (86-103): name, script, interpreter, args, cwd, build, port, enabled
- **BotMenuConfig**: default items + per-user personalized menus (千人千面)
- **ProxyConfig**: http proxy URL and noProxy list
- **EmbeddingConfig**: provider, apiKey, model, dimensions
- **GoogleConfig**: apiKey, model
- **TracingConfig**: enabled, logsDir, tracesDir, retentionDays

---

### **2. Config Loading Flow** (`loadConfig()` function, lines 289-437)

**Priority Order** (highest to lowest):
1. **Environment variables** (REMI_*, FEISHU_*, BYTEDANCE_*, GOOGLE_API_KEY)
2. **remi.toml file** (parsed via smol-toml)
3. **Defaults** (hardcoded in defaultRemiConfig())

**Search Path for remi.toml**:
- `./remi.toml` (current working directory)
- `~/.remi/remi.toml` (home directory)

**Environment Variables Used**:
- Provider: `REMI_PROVIDER`, `REMI_FALLBACK`, `REMI_MODEL`, `REMI_TIMEOUT`, `REMI_API_KEY`, `REMI_BASE_URL`
- Feishu: `FEISHU_APP_ID`, `FEISHU_APP_SECRET`, `FEISHU_VERIFICATION_TOKEN`, `FEISHU_ENCRYPT_KEY`, `FEISHU_PORT`, `FEISHU_DOMAIN`, `FEISHU_USER_ACCESS_TOKEN`
- ByteDance SSO: `BYTEDANCE_SSO_CLIENT_ID`, `BYTEDANCE_SSO_HOST`, `BYTEDANCE_BYTECLOUD_HOST`
- Other: `REMI_MEMORY_DIR`, `REMI_LOG_LEVEL`, `REMI_HEARTBEAT`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`

**TOML Section Mapping** (snake_case in file → camelCase in object):
- `[provider]` → provider config
- `[feishu]` → feishu config
- `[bytedance_sso]` → bytedanceSso config
- `[[token_sync]]` → tokenSync array
- `[scheduler]` → scheduler config (deprecated)
- `[[scheduled_skills]]` → scheduledSkills array (deprecated)
- `[cron]` → cron jobs config
- `[[cron.jobs]]` → cronJobs array
- `[[services]]` → services array
- `[bot_menu]` → botMenu config
- `[proxy]` → proxy config
- `[embedding]` → embedding config
- `[google]` → google config
- `[tracing]` → tracing config

---

### **3. Default Values** (`defaultRemiConfig()` function, lines 259-283)

```typescript
- provider.name: "claude_cli"
- provider.timeout: 300 (seconds)
- feishu.port: 9000
- feishu.domain: "feishu"
- scheduler.memoryCompactCron: "0 3 * * *"
- scheduler.heartbeatInterval: 300 (seconds)
- memoryDir: ~/.remi/memory
- pidFile: ~/.remi/remi.pid
- logLevel: "INFO"
- contextWarnThreshold: 6000
- queueDir: ~/.remi/queue
- sessionsFile: ~/.remi/sessions.json
- tracing.enabled: true
- tracing.logsDir: ~/.remi/logs
- tracing.tracesDir: ~/.remi/traces
- tracing.retentionDays: 60
```

---

### **4. Config Consumption Across Codebase**

**Core Initialization** (`/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/core.ts`):
- Line 76: `config: RemiConfig` stored in Remi class
- Lines 864-943: `Remi.boot(config)` static method:
  - Creates AuthStore with token sync rules
  - Registers Feishu auth adapter if credentials present
  - Registers ByteDance SSO adapter if configured
  - Builds primary provider + fallback provider
  - Registers FeishuConnector with menu syncing
  - Initializes SymlinkManager

**CLI Entry Points**:
- `/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/cli/serve.ts` (line 18): `loadConfig()` for daemon mode
  - Calls `migrateConfigFile()` to convert legacy [scheduler] → [[cron.jobs]]
  - Calls `migrateToCronJobs(config)` for effective cron jobs list
- `/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/cli/chat.ts` (line 13): Interactive CLI REPL
- `/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/cli/pm2-commands.ts` (line 13): PM2 service management

**Feishu Connector** (`/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/connectors/feishu/index.ts`):
- Receives FeishuConfig in constructor
- Uses appId, appSecret, domain for authentication
- Uses port for WebSocket listener
- Uses triggerUserIds to control reply behavior
- Uses verificationToken, encryptKey for message validation

**Provider Building** (`/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/core.ts`, lines 945-958):
- Extracts provider.model, provider.timeout, provider.allowedTools
- Passes apiKey, baseUrl to provider constructors

**PM2 Integration** (`/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/pm2.ts`):
- Lines 21-32: Builds proxy environment from config.proxy
- Lines 40-77: Generates ecosystem.config.cjs from services array

**Scheduler/Cron** (`/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/queue/handlers/cron-bridge.ts`):
- Registers cron handlers based on cronJobs config
- Handler config passed to specific job implementations

---

### **5. Config Migration & Validation**

**Legacy Migration** (lines 445-485):
- Function `migrateToCronJobs()` converts old [scheduler] + [[scheduled_skills]] to new [[cron.jobs]]
- Function `migrateConfigFile()` (lines 497-543):
  - Detects old format
  - Backs up original as remi.toml.bak
  - Rewrites file with new [[cron.jobs]] format
  - Called automatically on startup in serve mode

**Validation Checks** (Doctor command, `/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/cli/doctor.ts`):
- Checks for remi.toml existence
- Validates Feishu app_id + app_secret presence
- Verifies required runtime dependencies

**Config File Management** (`/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/cli/toml-writer.ts`):
- `setConfigValue(section, key, value)`: Safe TOML manipulation
- `getConfigValue(section, key)`: Read individual values
- `ensureConfigFile(template)`: Initialize default config

---

### **6. Key File Paths**

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/config.ts` | Config definitions and loading |
| `/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/core.ts` | Remi.boot() uses config for component assembly |
| `/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/cli/serve.ts` | Daemon mode - loads config + migrates legacy |
| `/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/cli/chat.ts` | REPL mode - loads config |
| `/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/pm2.ts` | Uses config.services + config.proxy |
| `/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/cli/doctor.ts` | Validates config existence |
| `/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/cli/toml-writer.ts` | Safe TOML manipulation |
| `/data00/home/hehuajie/project/remi/src/connectors/feishu/index.ts` | Uses FeishuConfig for setup |

---

### **7. Resolution Flow Diagram**

```
User invokes CLI
    ↓
main.ts → dispatch() → cli/serve.ts or cli/chat.ts
    ↓
loadConfig(configPath?)
    ├─ Parse remi.toml from: ./remi.toml or ~/.remi/remi.toml
    ├─ Merge with process.env (REMI_*, FEISHU_*, etc.)
    └─ Apply defaults from defaultRemiConfig()
    ↓
config: RemiConfig object
    ↓
Remi.boot(config)
    ├─ Create AuthStore + adapters (Feishu, ByteDance SSO)
    ├─ Build Provider(s) from config.provider
    ├─ Create FeishuConnector from config.feishu
    ├─ Sync bot menu from config.botMenu
    └─ Initialize SymlinkManager
    ↓
remi.start() / remi.queue.start()
    ├─ setupSchedulers(cronJobs, remi) registers cron handlers
    └─ FeishuConnector.start() binds to config.feishu.port
```

---

### **Summary**

The config system is **modular, environment-aware, and backward-compatible**:
- **Single source of truth**: remi.toml (with env var overrides)
- **Three-tier priority**: env vars > file > defaults
- **Automatic migration**: Legacy [scheduler] → new [[cron.jobs]]
- **Type-safe**: Full TypeScript interfaces with clear defaults
- **Lazy initialization**: Config loaded once at startup via loadConfig()
- **Centralized component assembly**: Remi.boot(config) wires all components
